ci_support_linux_64_c_stdlib_version2.17:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cuda_compiler:
- cuda-nvcc
cuda_compiler_version:
- '12.9'
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  python:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
python:
- 3.10.* *_cpython
pytorch:
- '2.10'
target_platform:
- linux-64
zip_keys:
- - c_compiler_version
  - cxx_compiler_version
  - c_stdlib_version
  - cuda_compiler_version

about.summary: CUDA accelerated rasterization of gaussian splatting
CUDA accelerated rasterization of gaussian splatting
CUDA accelerated rasterization of gaussian splatting

name: gsplat
raw_meta_yaml:
schema_version: 1

context:
  name: gsplat
  version: "1.5.3"
  build_number: 5

package:
  name: ${{ name|lower }}
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  - url: |
      https://github.com/nerfstudio-project/${{ name }}/archive/v${{ version }}.tar.gz
    sha256: 8a24428b8ea2ce7c3e10fcf5aa20e20fe503b8329c96db797f4eab703729aac3
    patches:
      - patches/0001-Remove-ninja-from-install-requirements.patch
      - patches/0002-Remove-vendored-glm-from-include-dirs.patch
      - patches/0003-Fix-pytest-on-python-313.patch

build:
  number: ${{ build_number }}
  script:
    - export MAX_JOBS="3"
    # The following should match all the archs supported by conda-forge's pytorch,
    # including the +PTX modifier added to the most recent version.
    # pixi exec --spec "pytorch=*=cuda12*" python -c "import torch; print(torch.cuda.get_arch_list())"
    # pixi exec --spec "pytorch=*=cuda13*" python -c "import torch; print(torch.cuda.get_arch_list())"
    - if: match(cuda_compiler_version, "12.*")
      then: export TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST="7.5;8.0;8.6;9.0;10.0;12.0+PTX"
    - if: match(cuda_compiler_version, "13.*")
      then: export TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST="7.5;8.0;8.6;9.0;10.0;11.0;12.0+PTX"
    - export CUDA_HOME="$PREFIX"
    - $PYTHON -m pip install . -vv --no-build-isolation --no-deps
  skip:
    - cuda_compiler_version == "None"
    - osx
    - win
  # During the build, the dependency scanner may report libcudart from
  # `targets/<arch>/lib` because conda-forge CUDA keeps the real libraries there
  # and symlinks them into `<prefix>/lib`. After packaging, the final ELF is
  # correctly relocated: DT_NEEDED is libcudart.so.12 and the RPATH resolves
  # to `<prefix>/lib` via `$ORIGIN/../../..`.
  # dynamic_linking:
  #   overlinking_behavior: error
  #   overdepending_behavior: error

requirements:
  build:
    - ${{ stdlib("c") }}
    - ${{ compiler("c") }}
    - ${{ compiler("cxx") }}
    - ${{ compiler("cuda") }}
    - cmake
    - ninja
    - if: target_platform != build_platform
      then:
        - pip
        - pybind11
        - python
        - pytorch
        - setuptools
  host:
    # The build process complains about missing cicc binary in the host environment.
    # That binary is part of the CUDA compiler, therefore we need to have it also here in the host section.
    # The c and cxx compilers are needed by the cuda compiler.
    - ${{ compiler("c") }}
    - ${{ compiler("cxx") }}
    - ${{ compiler("cuda") }}
    # Host dependencies.
    - cuda-version ${{ cuda_compiler_version }}.*
    - cuda-cudart-dev
    - glm
    - jaxtyping
    - rich >=12
    - pip
    - python
    - pytorch
    - setuptools
  run:
    - jaxtyping
    - numpy
    - python
    - pytorch
    - rich >=12
    - typing_extensions

tests:
  - python:
      imports:
        - gsplat
      pip_check: true
  # Skipping aarch64 since the nerfacc conda package does not support this architecture yet.
  # Skipping also testing CUDA13 on linux-64 since nerfacc does not support it yet.
  - if: not aarch64 and (cuda_compiler_version != "None" and match(cuda_compiler_version, "<13"))
    then:
      - script:
          # Install these test deps directly from PyPI since they are not yet packaged in conda-forge.
          - |
            python -m pip install \
              torchpq \
              git+https://github.com/fraunhoferhhi/PLAS.git
          # These tests run only if a GPU is available.
          # Mainly used when the recipe is built locally since CI has no GPUs.
          - pytest tests/
        files:
          source:
            - assets/
            - tests/
        requirements:
          run:
            - cupy
            - imageio
            - nerfacc
            - pip
            - pytest
            - python
            # lapjv (plas dependency) has no wheel for Python 3.12 and 3.13.
            # Building the sdist requires a compiler.
            # https://pypi.org/project/lapjv/
            - ${{ compiler("cxx") }}

about:
  summary: CUDA accelerated rasterization of gaussian splatting
  license: Apache-2.0
  license_file: LICENSE
  homepage: https://github.com/nerfstudio-project/gsplat

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - jeongseok-meta
